Molly Realty
& Leisure
Brand Identity

Designing a dual-purpose visual identity that speaks to both property investors and nature-seeking travellers — from a single, unified logo mark.

Molly Realty and Leisure Ltd
Graphic & Brand Identity Designer
Adobe Illustrator, Photoshop
Logo, Colour System, Typography, Business Card
01
Project Overview

One Brand.
Two Personalities.

Molly Realty and Leisure Ltd operates across two distinct business verticals — real estate (buying, selling, and renting property) and agro-tourism (farm stays and nature-based leisure experiences). The challenge wasn't just designing a logo. It was designing a single visual identity that could speak credibly to two completely different audiences without feeling split or inconsistent.

Property investors expect a brand that communicates stability, trust, and professionalism. Leisure tourists want warmth, nature, and a sense of adventure. Most brands in this position create two separate sub-brand systems. The brief asked for one.

2
Brand verticals unified
5
Deliverables completed
1
Unified mark

Key Deliverables

Primary Logo Logo Variations Horizontal Layout Vertical Layout Monogram Brand Colour Palette Typography Selection Business Card Design
02
The Design Brief

Understanding the
Challenge

The Core Problem

The client needed a logo and brand identity that could visually represent two distinct business facets: real estate and agro-tourism. The visual identity had to be professional and trustworthy for real estate clients, while also feeling welcoming and natural for leisure and tourism.

🎯

Design challenge: Create a single emblem that carries dual meaning — home/property AND journey/nature — without forcing the viewer to choose which one they're looking at.

Target Audiences

  • Real estate side — individuals and families seeking to buy, sell, or rent property. They need to trust the brand instantly.
  • Leisure side — families, tourists, and adventure seekers interested in farm stays and nature-based activities. They need to feel invited.
  • Both audiences needed to feel the brand was built for them — not a compromise between two separate identities.
03
Process & Iteration

From Sketch
to Symbol

Ideation & Sketching

The initial phase focused on visually unifying two disparate concepts: Realty (security, structure, home) and Leisure (journey, path, nature). Early sketches explored abstract combinations of a house silhouette and a winding road.

The key insight came from working with negative space and line work to incorporate the brand initials — "M" and "R" — which helped evolve the house structure into a unique, memorable mark. The roof of the house becomes the letterform; the road curves out from its base like a path leading somewhere.

✏️

Design rationale: The final mark functions as a cohesive symbol with dual meaning. The overall shape reads as a sturdy house/shield for trust and security, while the path element communicates journey and destination — both simultaneously.

Colour Palette & Symbolism

Primary Green
#15381C · Deep Forest Green

Communicates growth, stability, trust, and nature — perfectly bridging land/property and agro-tourism.

Secondary Gold
#CEA52B · Mustard Yellow/Gold

Adds luxury, warmth, and high-quality leisure — suggesting a premium experience and financial success.

Typography System

MOLLY

Trajan Pro Bold

Brand name. Classic, established, authoritative. Instils confidence — key for real estate.

REALTY AND
LEISURE LTD

Quicksand

Descriptor. Clean, geometric sans-serif. Modern contrast that adds a fresh, inviting tone for the leisure side.

"The colour choice was critical to balancing the brand's dual personality — a palette that grounds the brand in trust and natural growth, while adding a layer of luxury and warmth."

04
Final Showcase

The Mark in
the Wild

The final brand system was applied across all key touchpoints — from digital to print. The logo system includes four variants: full colour, reversed/white, monochrome black, and monogram — ensuring the brand remains consistent and recognisable across every medium and background context.

Logo Variants Delivered

  • Full colour (green + gold) — Primary logo for use on white and light backgrounds across digital and print
  • Reversed (white) — For use on the primary green brand colour, envelopes, and dark backgrounds
  • Monochrome black — For single-colour print applications and embossing
  • Monogram mark — Icon-only version for small formats, favicons, and social media profile images

Business Card Design

The business card design uses the die-cut edge treatment to echo the unique shape of the logo mark itself — a deliberate decision that makes the card feel like an extension of the brand rather than a plain rectangle with a logo dropped on it. The deep forest green front with gold typography commands attention, while the reverse organises contact information in a clean, legible hierarchy.

05
Takeaways & Reflections

What This Project
Taught Me

Brand identity work for dual-purpose businesses is one of the most demanding briefs in graphic design. There's nowhere to hide a weak concept — the mark either carries both meanings or it doesn't. This project reinforced several principles that now sit at the core of how I approach identity work.

🏛️

Constraints breed creativity

The hardest constraint — one mark, two meanings — produced the most memorable solution. The negative space road-within-a-house only exists because the brief demanded it.

🎨

Colour is strategic, not aesthetic

Green and gold weren't chosen because they look nice together. Each was chosen for what it communicates to a specific audience — trust vs. warmth, nature vs. luxury.

✒️

Typography carries personality

Using two distinct typefaces to signal the two sides of the business name was a deliberate system decision — not a mixing of fonts. Hierarchy communicates meaning before the user reads a word.

📐

Sketch first, always

The final mark came from pencil-and-paper sketches, not a screen. The ideation phase — exploring negative space, letterform integration, and dual symbolism — could not have happened digitally first.

🃏

Collateral extends the concept

The die-cut business card wasn't a decorative choice — it was the logo mark made physical. Every brand touchpoint is an opportunity to reinforce the core concept.

🔄

Variants are non-negotiable

A logo that only works on white backgrounds isn't a complete brand. Delivering four variants from day one ensures the client can use the identity confidently across every medium.

"The design successfully solved the challenge by creating a single, memorable emblem that visually communicates both 'home' and 'travel/leisure' — a dual meaning that positions Molly as a versatile and trustworthy brand."

Crafting brands and digital experiences with intention.
